goal: Fix the critical pre-Phase-B safety gap: the real Builder write adapter currently hard-codes G-Dislioglu/soulmatch and ignores the resolved target profile. Thread the resolved target repo from orchestrateTask into smartPush and down to the patch/write adapter, without opening the Maya kill-switch, without flipping any target profile, without adding a real-write endpoint, and without performing any live write.
scope_out: Do not open, set, document, or depend on MAYA_BUILDER_REPO_WRITE_ENABLED.
Do not change any target profile's pushAllowed or writePolicy; bluepilot-sandbox stays dry_run_only.
Do not add a real-write trigger endpoint or modify server.ts.
Do not modify Maya gate, providers, patch mode, target profiles, dry-run probe, or sandbox-write probe.
Do not change the default behavior: absent targetRepo must still resolve to G-Dislioglu/soulmatch.
Do not perform a live GitHub write.
Do not log, return, or commit any token value.
claims: SmartPushOptions gains an optional targetRepo string in owner/name form.
smartPush validates targetRepo before using it; malformed targetRepo fails before any write or raw-content fetch for that target.
When targetRepo is provided, smartPush uses its owner/name for raw-content fallback reads and direct applyPatch writes.
When targetRepo is absent, smartPush uses the existing default G-Dislioglu/soulmatch behavior.
orchestrateTask passes targetProfile.repo as targetRepo at the single smartPush call site.
No real write path is made reachable by this task because target profiles, dryRun behavior, endpoints, and kill-switches are unchanged.
Tests prove targetRepo routing, default fallback, and malformed target rejection without making network or GitHub writes.
assumptions: applyPatch already accepts repoOwner and repoName parameters, so opusPatchMode.ts does not need to change.
orchestrateTask already has targetProfile.repo at the smartPush call site.
The sandbox profile remains pushAllowed:false after this task, so effectiveDryRun stays true for sandbox.
The first real guarded sandbox write is a later task after this pass-through is verified.
dod: smartPush accepts targetRepo and threads the parsed owner/name to direct applyPatch writes and raw-content fallback reads.
smartPush without targetRepo still routes to G-Dislioglu/soulmatch, proven by test.
orchestrateTask passes targetProfile.repo as targetRepo at the existing smartPush call site, with no other orchestrator logic change.
Malformed targetRepo is rejected before any write or target-specific network call, proven by test.
Source check: no MAYA_BUILDER_REPO_WRITE_ENABLED, no pushAllowed change, no new endpoint, no dryRun change in this diff.
npm run typecheck in builder/ passes.
stop_conditions: A live write would be required to prove the change.
A target profile, endpoint, Maya gate, provider, patch mode, or package change becomes necessary.
The default soulmatch fallback cannot be preserved.
A secret value would need to be logged, returned, or committed.
Opening the Maya kill-switch becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: BP-139 proved/introduced a sandbox write-readiness probe but also documented the safety gap: current SmartPush is hard-coded to G-Dislioglu/soulmatch. Claude's first BP-140 proposal would have flipped the sandbox profile and opened the write path while SmartPush still targeted soulmatch. Codex stopped that as a hard-stop. This BP-140 is the corrected pass-through-only prerequisite.
